examples.title: Algorithm: Erosion
examples.description: Erodes an image by a structuring element
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Erosion
INPUT: image, structuringElement
OUTPUT: erodedImage

BEGIN
  FOR each pixel in image
    IF structuringElement fits entirely within image at pixel
      THEN erodedImage[pixel] = 1
    ELSE erodedImage[pixel] = 0
  END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the erosion operation. It iterates over each pixel in the image and checks if the structuring element fits entirely within the image at that pixel. If it does, the corresponding pixel in the eroded image is set to 1; otherwise, it is set to 0.

examples.title: Python: Erosion
examples.description: Erodes an image by a structuring element
examples.explanation: This Python function implements the erosion operation. It uses NumPy arrays to represent the image and structuring element, and iterates over each pixel in the image to check if the structuring element fits entirely within the image at that pixel.

examples.title: C: Dilation
examples.description: Dilates an image by a structuring element
examples.explanation: This C function implements the dilation operation. It uses nested loops to iterate over each pixel in the image and checks if the structuring element overlaps with the image at that pixel.

examples.title: Java: Grayscale Erosion
examples.description: Erodes a grayscale image by a structuring element
examples.explanation: This Java function implements the grayscale erosion operation. It uses nested loops to iterate over each pixel in the image and finds the minimum value of the pixels covered by the structuring element.
